title: Nairobi River flash flood — March 2026

hazard.caption: RIM2D inundation footprint — Nairobi, 6 Mar 2026 (illustrative hazard extent)

brief_outcome_free: Nairobi admin-1, early March long-rains onset. Dense informal settlements along the Nairobi/Mathare/Ngong river corridors are exposed to flash flooding. Ground is already wet from late-February rain. You are the DOC duty analyst on Mar 1.

peak.description: Nairobi River flash flood overnight 6-7 Mar

rounds.title: Early monitoring (T-5)
rounds.engine_state: Moderate risk / Evaluate (Yellow) — saturated ground, mean forecast still modest

rounds.title: Forecast escalation (T-2)
rounds.engine_state: Moderate risk / Assess (Orange) — tail ratio 1.11 crosses 2-yr RP, 2-day lead

rounds.title: Observed confirmation (T0)
rounds.engine_state: High risk / Actionable_Risk (Red) — flood onset; DBN carries the Mar 4 signal forward

evidence_cards.label: Ensemble-mean exceedance prob (ECMWF P_heavy)
evidence_cards.source: ECMWF 51-member TP, P(any member >= 2-yr RP), mean
evidence_cards.teaching_note: The MEAN looks benign. Do not stop here.

evidence_cards.label: Ensemble-max / 2-yr RP, pixel p95
evidence_cards.teaching_note: Mean ~18 mm vs worst member 131 mm. Only the tail fires. THIS is the signal a mean-threshold system misses.

evidence_cards.label: Yesterday's risk posterior carried forward (DBN, alpha=0.6)
evidence_cards.teaching_note: Temporal coupling = virtual evidence. It is why Mar 6 stays elevated.

act1_quiz.prompt: On 1 March 2026 the 7-day antecedent rainfall over Nairobi is about 135 mm and the ground is saturated, while the ensemble-mean forecast still looks modest. Why does the antecedent evidence alone justify stepping up vigilance?
act1_quiz.hint: The brief notes the ground is already wet from late-February rain.
options: It does not; a modest mean forecast means low risk regardless of soil state
Saturated ground converts almost any further intense storm directly into runoff, so the same forecast rain is far more dangerous than it would be on dry ground
Wet soil mainly increases drought risk later in the season
Antecedent rainfall only matters for large rural river basins, not cities
act1_quiz.correct: Saturated ground converts almost any further intense storm directly into runoff, so the same forecast rain is far more dangerous than it would be on dry ground
act1_quiz.answer_note: With soils saturated, infiltration capacity is gone and new rainfall runs straight into Nairobi's small, fast-responding river corridors. That is why the scenario starts at elevated vigilance even while the ensemble-mean forecast is still benign.
act1_quiz.bn_purpose: Why the antecedent_rainfall node shifts the flood prior before any forecast node fires.

act1_quiz.prompt: At T-2 (4 March 2026) the ensemble mean is only about 18 mm, but the worst ensemble member gives 131 mm and the p95 ensemble-max ratio crosses the 2-year return period (1.11). What is the correct reading of this split?
act1_quiz.hint: Mean and tail are answering different questions.
options: The mean is the best estimate, so the heavy members can be discarded as noise
A minority of members produce an extreme convective storm: the mean hides a real tail risk that a mean-threshold system would miss, so precautionary action at 2-day lead is justified
The disagreement means the forecast is unusable and no action should be taken
The tail member guarantees a flood will occur on 6 March
act1_quiz.correct: A minority of members produce an extreme convective storm: the mean hides a real tail risk that a mean-threshold system would miss, so precautionary action at 2-day lead is justified
act1_quiz.answer_note: Convective flash-flood rainfall is exactly the kind of event where only some ensemble members capture the storm, so the mean stays low while the tail crosses the damage threshold. The tail does not guarantee an event; it quantifies a credible worst case with enough lead time for low-cost, no-regret action along the river corridors.
act1_quiz.bn_purpose: Why the tail_risk node, not exceedance_prob alone, is the decisive evidence for tail-forecastability events.

decision.checkpoint_prompt: At T-2 (Mar 4) the ensemble MEAN is benign but the tail crosses the 2-yr RP. What DOC level do you set, and why? A 'Watch + 24h re-check + note the tail uncertainty' is a strong answer.

counterfactual.prompt: What if a precautionary no-regret action (alert river-corridor focal points, pre-position) were taken at T-2 on the tail signal?
counterfactual.narrative: Acting on the 2-day-lead tail signal — even under a benign ensemble mean — is exactly the lead time CRMA's tail_risk node exists to surface. The counterfactual illustrates loss reduction without claiming certainty.

debrief.loss_note_2026: Debrief links to the curated March 2026 Nairobi flood storyline (RK key 2026-IBF01-KEN): >=108-112 deaths, 34,765 displaced across 30 counties; Nairobi River burst 6 Mar 2026.
